verses.text: Zedekiah sends to Jeremiah for prayer as the Chaldeans withdraw before Pharaoh's army. He seeks help in crisis. Men turn to God's prophet when their fears press hard.

God answers that Pharaoh will retreat and the Chaldeans return to burn the city; even wounded men would rise to do it. The reprieve is brief. No alliance can avert the judgment God has decreed.

Leaving the city, Jeremiah is seized as a deserter, beaten, and imprisoned by the angry princes. He suffers false charges. The faithful are often treated as traitors for telling the truth.

Zedekiah secretly asks if there is a word; Jeremiah says plainly, 'Thou shalt be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon.' He does not soften it. Even in prison the prophet speaks the truth to the king.

Zedekiah secretly asks if there is a word; Jeremiah says plainly, 'Thou shalt be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon.' He does not soften it. Even in prison the prophet speaks the truth to the king.
Jeremiah pleads not to be returned to the dungeon to die; the king grants him the prison court and a daily loaf. A small mercy. God provides for His servant even in confinement.
